basic_details.job_title: Indian Army NCC Special Entry 125 Course (Women) Notification Out: Eligibility, Salary, Apply Online
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Application → Shortlisting → SSB → Medical → Merit List → Joining Letter
basic_details.short_information: The Indian Army has invited online applications from unmarried women holding an NCC 'C' Certificate, and Wards of Battle Casualties of Indian Army personnel, for Short Service Commission (Non-Technical) under the NCC Special Entry Scheme, 125th Course (April 2027) - SSCW (NCC)-125. This article covers complete details on eligibility, vacancies, selection process, training, and salary as per the official notification.

important_dates.raw: Online application opens: 21 July 2026, 1500 hrs Online application closes: 21 August 2026, 1500 hrs SSB shortlisting (tentative): First week of September 2026 SSB Centre and date selection window: 14 days in September 2026 (first-come-first-served) SSB interviews (tentative): November/December 2026, maximum 5 days Course commencement: April 2027 Deadline to submit final degree certificate (final-year candidates): 01 April 2027

age_limit.details: Age limit is detailed above. Candidates must also meet minimum physical standards before joining the training academy:

qualification.education_qualification: Graduation degree and minimum 'B' Grade in NCC 'C' Certificate exam
qualification.details: The candidate must be: (i) a citizen of India, or (ii) a subject of Nepal, or (iii) a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, or specified East African countries (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Zambia, Malawi, Zaire, Ethiopia) and Vietnam, with the intention of permanently settling in India, provided a certificate of eligibility has been issued by the Government of India (not required for Gorkha subjects of Nepal). | 19 to 25 years as on 01 January 2027 (born not earlier than 02 January 2002 and not later than 01 January 2008, both dates inclusive). The date of birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate at the time of application will be treated as final; no subsequent change request will be entertained. | Minimum qualification: a Bachelor's degree or equivalent from a recognized university. Final-year candidates may apply, provided they complete their degree before the course begins; the aggregate percentage from the first two/three years of the degree course will be considered for shortlisting. Minimum two/three years of service (as applicable) in the Senior Division/Wing of NCC. Minimum 'B' Grade in the NCC 'C' Certificate exam. Candidates without an NCC 'C' Certificate (or only a Provisional NCC 'C' Certificate) on the date of application are not eligible. Provisional certificates are not accepted at the SSB stage. | Important: Candidates may appear for only one SSB interview - either as a CDSE candidate for SSCW (NT) (Women)-125 Course, or for NCC (Special) Entry-125 Course (Women). An undertaking to this effect must be given in the online application. | Available to unmarried sons and unmarried daughters (including legally adopted) of Army personnel classified as: killed in action; died of wounds/injuries (other than self-inflicted); wounded/injured (other than self-inflicted); or missing, presumed dead. Educational qualification: Graduate degree from a recognized university (final-year candidates also eligible under the same conditions as above). NCC 'C' Certificate is not required for this category. Required documents: Battle Casualty certificate number issued by the Manpower (Policy & Planning) Directorate (or Adjutant General's Branch MP-5(D) for casualties after September 2013); copy of Part II Order classifying the casualty as a "Battle Casualty"; and a Relationship Certificate issued by the Manpower Directorate/Regimental Records.

vacancy_details.Category: Wards of Battle Casualties of Indian Army Personnel

category_wise_breakdown.category: Wards of Battle Casualties of Indian Army Personnel

salary_details.details: Stipend during training: ₹56,100 per month (fixed at OTA, Chennai for the full 49-week duration). Military Service Pay (MSP): ₹15,500 per month, fixed, for officers from Lieutenant to Brigadier. Approximate CTC on commissioning: ₹17–18 lakh per annum, excluding free medical cover and one home-town visit per year. Additional allowances include Dearness Allowance, Transport Allowance, Children Education Allowance (₹2,250/month per child, for two eldest children), Hostel Subsidy (₹6,750/month per child), Dress Allowance (₹20,000/year), and various field/hardship allowances depending on posting.

selection_process: Shortlisting of applications: Based on bonus marks out of a maximum of 100 - up to 50 marks for NCC 'C' Certificate grading (50 for 'A' Grade

25 for 'B' Grade) and up to 50 marks for graduation performance (0 to 50 marks on a sliding scale from below 60% to 85%+ aggregate). Vacancy-to-call-up ratio: Candidates are shortlisted for SSB based on this ratio

decided by the Directorate General of Recruiting. SSB Centre and date selection: Shortlisted candidates choose their SSB centre and date on a first-come-first-served basis; choices cannot be changed later except under exceptional circumstances. SSB interview: Conducted over 5 days at Prayagraj
or Jalandhar. Candidates undergo a two-stage process - Stage I

and Stage II (for those who clear Stage I). Candidates who fail Stage I are sent back the same day. Medical examination: Conducted for candidates recommended after Stage II. Merit list: Prepared based on SSB marks. Ties are broken first by age (older candidate ranked higher)
then by percentage in the qualifying exam. Joining letter: Issued to candidates within the merit list and available vacancies
subject to meeting all eligibility criteria and being declared medically fit.
Candidates who fail to report within 7 days of the reporting date will have their candidature cancelled
and the next candidate in the merit list will be considered.
how_to_apply: Visit www.joinindianarmy.nic.in and click "Officer Entry Application/Login," then "Registration" (skip if already registered).
After registration, click "Apply Online" under the Dashboard, then click "Apply" against Short Service Commission NCC Special Entry Course.
Fill in personal information, communication details, education details, and previous SSB details (if any), clicking "Save & Continue" after each section.
Review the summary of your information, then click "Submit" only after confirming all details are correct.
Take two printouts of the application (with roll number) 30 minutes after the online application closes on the last day.
No changes can be made to the application after it closes. Candidates must submit only one application - multiple applications will result in cancellation.
important_instructions: Candidate's name, parents' names, and date of birth in the profile and application must exactly match the Matriculation/Secondary certificate; any variation leads to cancellation. CGPA/grades must be converted to marks per the university's formula; discrepancies found later lead to cancellation. Candidates cannot marry during training, nor live with parents/guardians during the training period; marrying before or during training leads to removal and recovery of training costs. Cost of training at OTA is borne by the Government, except for candidates withdrawn for personal reasons or reasons within their control, who must refund training costs at ₹20,482 per week (as of 2026). Candidates serving in the Armed Forces must inform their Commanding Officer in writing about applying, and carry a countersigned application to the SSB. Candidates should never have been debarred by UPSC from any exam, arrested/convicted by a criminal court, or expelled from any service training academy on disciplinary grounds. Travelling Allowance (AC 3-Tier fare) is admissible only for the first SSB appearance for a particular type of commission. Successful completion of training at OTA, Chennai earns a Post Graduate Diploma in Defence Management and Strategic Studies from the University of Madras.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army NCC Special Entry Scheme 125: Quick Overview

rows.details: NCC Special Entry Scheme – 125th Course (April 2027), Short Service Commission (NT), Women (including Wards of Battle Casualties)
Graduation degree and minimum 'B' Grade in NCC 'C' Certificate exam
Application → Shortlisting → SSB → Medical → Merit List → Joining Letter

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Online application opens: 21 July 2026, 1500 hrs Online application closes: 21 August 2026, 1500 hrs SSB shortlisting (tentative): First week of September 2026 SSB Centre and date selection window: 14 days in September 2026 (first-come-first-served) SSB interviews (tentative): November/December 2026, maximum 5 days Course commencement: April 2027 Deadline to submit final degree certificate (final-year candidates): 01 April 2027

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The Indian Army notifies vacancies separately for each entry and gender based on operational and administrative requirements. The shortlisting process, cut-offs, and merit for men and women are independent of each other.

rows.category: Wards of Battle Casualties of Indian Army Personnel

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: The candidate must be: (i) a citizen of India, or (ii) a subject of Nepal, or (iii) a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, or specified East African countries (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Zambia, Malawi, Zaire, Ethiopia) and Vietnam, with the intention of permanently settling in India, provided a certificate of eligibility has been issued by the Government of India (not required for Gorkha subjects of Nepal). 19 to 25 years as on 01 January 2027 (born not earlier than 02 January 2002 and not later than 01 January 2008, both dates inclusive). The date of birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate at the time of application will be treated as final; no subsequent change request will be entertained. Minimum qualification: a Bachelor's degree or equivalent from a recognized university. Final-year candidates may apply, provided they complete their degree before the course begins; the aggregate percentage from the first two/three years of the degree course will be considered for shortlisting. Minimum two/three years of service (as applicable) in the Senior Division/Wing of NCC. Minimum 'B' Grade in the NCC 'C' Certificate exam. Candidates without an NCC 'C' Certificate (or only a Provisional NCC 'C' Certificate) on the date of application are not eligible. Provisional certificates are not accepted at the SSB stage. Important: Candidates may appear for only one SSB interview - either as a CDSE candidate for SSCW (NT) (Women)-125 Course, or for NCC (Special) Entry-125 Course (Women). An undertaking to this effect must be given in the online application. Available to unmarried sons and unmarried daughters (including legally adopted) of Army personnel classified as: killed in action; died of wounds/injuries (other than self-inflicted); wounded/injured (other than self-inflicted); or missing, presumed dead. Educational qualification: Graduate degree from a recognized university (final-year candidates also eligible under the same conditions as above). NCC 'C' Certificate is not required for this category. Required documents: Battle Casualty certificate number issued by the Manpower (Policy & Planning) Directorate (or Adjutant General's Branch MP-5(D) for casualties after September 2013); copy of Part II Order classifying the casualty as a "Battle Casualty"; and a Relationship Certificate issued by the Manpower Directorate/Regimental Records.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit and Physical Standards
content_sections.text: Age limit is detailed above. Candidates must also meet minimum physical standards before joining the training academy:

content_sections.heading: Salary and Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Stipend during training: ₹56,100 per month (fixed at OTA, Chennai for the full 49-week duration). Military Service Pay (MSP): ₹15,500 per month, fixed, for officers from Lieutenant to Brigadier. Approximate CTC on commissioning: ₹17–18 lakh per annum, excluding free medical cover and one home-town visit per year. Additional allowances include Dearness Allowance, Transport Allowance, Children Education Allowance (₹2,250/month per child, for two eldest children), Hostel Subsidy (₹6,750/month per child), Dress Allowance (₹20,000/year), and various field/hardship allowances depending on posting.

rows.promotion_criteria: On completion of 2 years reckonable commissioned service
On completion of 6 years reckonable commissioned service
On completion of 13 years reckonable commissioned service
On completion of 26 years reckonable commissioned service
On selection basis, subject to fulfilment of requisite service conditions

content_sections.heading: Tenure and Release Options
content_sections.text: Officers are liable to serve for 10 years, extendable by a further 4 years, subject to eligibility and organizational requirements. Permanent Commission may be considered in the 10th year of service. Officers may apply for release during the 5th year of service; Army HQ's decision on such applications is final. Release options are also available after the 10th and 14th years of service. Reserve liability: Officers released before completion of contractual service must serve the Army Reserve for 5 years, plus 2 years on a voluntary basis, or up to age 37 (whichever is earlier).

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Shortlisting of applications: Based on bonus marks out of a maximum of 100 - up to 50 marks for NCC 'C' Certificate grading (50 for 'A' Grade, 25 for 'B' Grade) and up to 50 marks for graduation performance (0 to 50 marks on a sliding scale from below 60% to 85%+ aggregate). Vacancy-to-call-up ratio: Candidates are shortlisted for SSB based on this ratio, decided by the Directorate General of Recruiting. SSB Centre and date selection: Shortlisted candidates choose their SSB centre and date on a first-come-first-served basis; choices cannot be changed later except under exceptional circumstances. SSB interview: Conducted over 5 days at Prayagraj, Bhopal, Bengaluru, or Jalandhar. Candidates undergo a two-stage process - Stage I, and Stage II (for those who clear Stage I). Candidates who fail Stage I are sent back the same day. Medical examination: Conducted for candidates recommended after Stage II. Merit list: Prepared based on SSB marks. Ties are broken first by age (older candidate ranked higher), then by percentage in the qualifying exam. Joining letter: Issued to candidates within the merit list and available vacancies, subject to meeting all eligibility criteria and being declared medically fit. Candidates who fail to report within 7 days of the reporting date will have their candidature cancelled, and the next candidate in the merit list will be considered.

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Signed printout of the online application with self-attested photograph Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate (or equivalent) and mark sheet 12th Class certificate and mark sheet Graduation degree or provisional degree certificate (provisional degree must not be older than 6 months as on 01 April 2027, i.e., issued between 01 October 2026 and 01 April 2027) Mark sheets of all years/semesters NCC 'C' Certificate, issued on or before the date of online application submission (not required for Wards of Battle Casualties) CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate from the university, where applicable For final-year candidates: certificate from the institution head confirming final-year status and expected result declaration by 01 April 2027 For Wards of Battle Casualties: Battle Casualty certificate, Part II Order copy, and Relationship Certificate Note: DigiLocker certificates will not be accepted in place of original certificates. All originals must be carried to the SSB interview; incomplete documents lead to summary rejection.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Visit www.joinindianarmy.nic.in and click "Officer Entry Application/Login," then "Registration" (skip if already registered). After registration, click "Apply Online" under the Dashboard, then click "Apply" against Short Service Commission NCC Special Entry Course. Fill in personal information, communication details, education details, and previous SSB details (if any), clicking "Save & Continue" after each section. Review the summary of your information, then click "Submit" only after confirming all details are correct. Take two printouts of the application (with roll number) 30 minutes after the online application closes on the last day. No changes can be made to the application after it closes. Candidates must submit only one application - multiple applications will result in cancellation.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidate's name, parents' names, and date of birth in the profile and application must exactly match the Matriculation/Secondary certificate; any variation leads to cancellation. CGPA/grades must be converted to marks per the university's formula; discrepancies found later lead to cancellation. Candidates cannot marry during training, nor live with parents/guardians during the training period; marrying before or during training leads to removal and recovery of training costs. Cost of training at OTA is borne by the Government, except for candidates withdrawn for personal reasons or reasons within their control, who must refund training costs at ₹20,482 per week (as of 2026). Candidates serving in the Armed Forces must inform their Commanding Officer in writing about applying, and carry a countersigned application to the SSB. Candidates should never have been debarred by UPSC from any exam, arrested/convicted by a criminal court, or expelled from any service training academy on disciplinary grounds. Travelling Allowance (AC 3-Tier fare) is admissible only for the first SSB appearance for a particular type of commission. Successful completion of training at OTA, Chennai earns a Post Graduate Diploma in Defence Management and Strategic Studies from the University of Madras.

faq.question: Q1. Who is eligible for the Indian Army NCC Special Entry Scheme 125th Course for women?
faq.answer: Unmarried female graduates aged 19–25 years (as on 01 January 2027) holding a minimum 'B' Grade in the NCC 'C' Certificate exam, along with 2–3 years of service in the Senior Division/Wing of NCC, are eligible. Wards of Battle Casualties of Indian Army personnel are also eligible, without the NCC 'C' Certificate requirement.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in this recruitment?
faq.answer: There are 6 vacancies in total - 5 under the General category and 1 reserved for Wards of Battle Casualties of Indian Army personnel.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for SSCW (NCC)-125?
faq.answer: The online application closes on 21 August 2026 at 1500 hrs. Applications opened on 21 July 2026 at 1500 hrs.

faq.question: Q4. What is the stipend during training?
faq.answer: Candidates receive a stipend of ₹56,100 per month during the 49-week training period at Officers Training Academy, Chennai.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary after commissioning?
faq.answer: On commissioning as a Lieutenant, the approximate CTC is ₹17–18 lakh per annum, excluding free medical cover and one home-town visit per year.

faq.question: Q6. Is the NCC 'C' Certificate mandatory for this entry?
faq.answer: Yes, for the general category - a minimum 'B' Grade in the NCC 'C' Certificate exam is required, and it must be obtained on or before the date of application. It is not required for Wards of Battle Casualties.

faq.question: Q7. Where will the SSB interview be conducted?
faq.answer: SSB interviews will be conducted at Prayagraj (Uttar Pradesh), Bhopal (Madhya Pradesh), Bengaluru (Karnataka), and Jalandhar (Punjab).

faq.question: Q8. Can a candidate apply for both SSCW (NT) and NCC Special Entry in the same course?
faq.answer: No. Candidates can appear for only one SSB interview - either as a CDSE candidate for SSCW (NT) (Women)-125 Course or for NCC (Special) Entry-125 Course (Women) - and must submit an undertaking to this effect in the online application.
